summary: Current Next.js stack already implements most legacy behaviors; gaps are mainly CLI ergonomics, explicit 20–25 batch defaults for imports, and quality-preserving dedupe rules.

import.dedupe: source-key, slug, title+body hash; optional --dedupe-report JSON; skips overwrite when existing content is longer/richer (preserveExistingOverIncoming)

generation.aiDraft: generateBlogAiDraft / generateBlogPost with template + intent + exam context

scheduling.batchSchedule: BlogBatchSchedule + BlogBatchScheduleItem with plannedPublishAt, stagger via cadencePerDay
scheduling.autoPublish: promoteScheduledBlogPosts flips SCHEDULED → PUBLISHED when due; retries capped

batch_generation.current: Yes (admin APIs + batch schedule + autopilot queue)

staggered_publish.gap: Cron max 12 items/invocation (by design); use more frequent cron for throughput

deduplication.current: Stronger (hash + slug + source key + quality gate)

seoDifferences.titles: Current supports metaTitleVariant, titleAlternates, featuredSnippet — imports map seoTitle/seoDescription from legacy meta fields
seoDifferences.internalLinks: Current persists relatedLessonPaths, relatedTools, internalLinkPlan; legacy imports preserve hrefs embedded in HTML
